confidence_note: confidence values are normalized floats from 0.0 to 1.0

claims.statement: Cost decline is structural, not cyclical — driven by manufacturing scale
claims.context: 10/10 analyses converged. Learning curve (22% cost reduction per doubling) has held for 40 years.

claims.statement: Grid parity already achieved in 75% of global markets
claims.context: IRENA/BloombergNEF data. Subsidy removal in mature markets has not reversed adoption.

claims.statement: Cost floor approaching — further declines will slow significantly
claims.context: Silicon material costs impose theoretical floor. 3/10 analyses supported this framing. Tension with claim 1 is informative — reveals the timeline question.

claims.statement: Bifacial modules now 70% of new installations globally
claims.context: Rear-side gain 5-25% depending on albedo. Bankability established. Monofacial declining to niche applications.

claims.statement: Perovskite tandem cells: 33.7% lab efficiency (Oxford PV)
claims.context: Lab-to-fab gap remains. Commercial viability estimated 2028-2030 for utility scale.

claims.statement: Durability is the unsolved risk for perovskite commercialization
claims.context: Moisture/heat degradation pathways known but not fully mitigated. 25-year warranty requirement is the hard gate.

claims.statement: Utility-scale solar is now the cheapest new electricity source globally
claims.context: LCOE below gas and wind in most markets. Subsidy-independent growth trajectory confirmed.

claims.statement: Supply chain concentration in China is the highest systemic risk
claims.context: 80% of polysilicon, 95% of wafers. Tariff/geopolitical disruption could create 12-18 month supply gaps. 8/10 analyses flagged this. Not a technology problem — a trade policy and diversification problem.

evidence.source: Internal analysis — 10 independent assessments across multiple AI models
evidence.description: Multi-source synthesis across 10 analyses and 8 distinct models. All converged on cost decline being structural (manufacturing learning curve) rather than cyclical (subsidy-driven).
Comprehensive dataset on utility-scale solar PV LCOE trends across 40 countries. Learning rate of 22% per capacity doubling confirmed across 1976-2025 data.

evidence.source: MIT Energy Initiative — The Future of Solar Energy (updated 2025)
evidence.description: Analysis of silicon cost floors and theoretical efficiency limits. Argues that polysilicon pricing has limited room for further reduction, implying LCOE declines will increasingly depend on balance-of-system and soft costs.

evidence.source: IEA Photovoltaic Power Systems Programme — Trends Report 2026
evidence.description: Global installed PV capacity figures and market share by technology type. Bifacial module share crossed 70% in Q4 2025.

evidence.source: Oxford PV press release — certified tandem efficiency record
evidence.description: 33.7% certified efficiency for perovskite-silicon tandem cell. Certification by Fraunhofer ISE CalLab. Commercial module target: 2028.

evidence.source: Nature Energy — "Stability challenges for perovskite photovoltaics" (2025)
evidence.description: Review of degradation mechanisms: moisture ingress, thermal cycling, UV exposure. Encapsulation advances extend lab lifetime to 5,000+ hours but field validation at 25-year scale is absent.

Accelerated aging tests on 12 encapsulation methods. Best-performing samples retained >90% efficiency at simulated 10-year equivalent. Gap to 25-year warranty requirement remains the commercialization gate.

evidence.source: BloombergNEF — Solar Supply Chain Vulnerability Index 2026
evidence.description: Quantitative assessment of geographic concentration in polysilicon, wafer, cell, and module production. China's share exceeds 80% at every stage except module assembly (65%). No diversification trend visible in 2025 data.
